root_text.sn54.20:1.2: Kathaṁ bhāvito ca, bhikkhave, ānāpānassatisamādhi kathaṁ bahulīkato saṁyojanappahānāya saṁvattati …
root_text.sn54.20:1.6: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā …pe…
root_text.sn54.20:1.7: paṭinissaggānupassī assasissāmīti sikkhati, paṭinissaggānupassī passasissāmīti sikkhati.
root_text.sn54.20:1.8: Evaṁ bhāvito kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassatisamādhi evaṁ bahulīkato saṁyojanappahānāya saṁvattati …pe…

translation_text.sn54.20:1.1: “Mendicants, when immersion due to mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated it leads to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.sn54.20:1.2: And how is immersion due to mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated so as to lead to giving up the fetters,
translation_text.sn54.20:1.4: completely understanding the course of time,
translation_text.sn54.20:1.6: It’s when a mendicant—gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut—sits down cross-legged, sets their body straight, and establishes mindfulness in front of them. …
translation_text.sn54.20:1.7: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing letting go.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing letting go.’
translation_text.sn54.20:1.8: That’s how immersion due to mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated so as to lead to giving up the fetters,
translation_text.sn54.20:1.10: completely understanding the course of time,
translation_text.sn54.20:2.5: The Linked Discourses on Mindfulness of Breathing is the tenth section.

reference_text.sn54.20:1.1: csp1ed14.297, csp2ed14.297, ms14S5_1785, msdiv996, pts-vp-pli5.341, vri27.409
